title: Inet datatype handling inconsistent between int, cast( as inet) and string
poc: insert into test_table values(cast('127.0.0.1' as inet));
select count(*) from test_table where col = '127.0.0.1';
select count(*) from test_table where col = inet '127.0.0.1';
select count(*) from test_table where col = cast('127.0.0.1' as inet);
create table test_table(pos int, col inet);
insert into test_table values(3, cast('127.0.0.1' as inet));
select * from test_table where col = inet '127.0.0.1';
select * from test_table where col = cast('127.0.0.1' as inet);
key_bug_features: Inconsistent inet datatype handling for different input formats
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table(col inet NOT NULL, id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY);
INSERT INTO test_table(col) VALUES(cast('192.168.1.3' AS inet));
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE col = '192.168.1.1';
